pages.full_text:
up to w[?alk] on the cliffs. Fabulous views.
        We drove to
    Fécamp, where
    there was a
    gambling casino
    but I couldn't
    find a postard.
Then we reached Yvetot,
We ate the delicious
    pastries with Tea +
    walked to the 1956
church, which apparently has
good glass, but we couldn't
get in. They were clearing up the market.
It rained. We left.
        We drove a short distance to a
secluded valley + reached
We booked a meal + decided to spend the
night because of the rain.
The garden was very pretty
with birdcages in + beech
hedges (+ gnomes!). The
pages.notes: Top-left text is partially obscured by a large pressed/dried straw/grass ornament glued to the page. The first line reads 'up to w[?alk] on the cliffs' — beginning is cut off, suggesting continuation from a previous page. A small photograph of coastal cliffs at sunset is glued in the lower-right area. The word 'postard' appears to be the diarist's spelling of 'postcard'. A place name appears to be missing after 'secluded valley + reached' — the line may continue on a line now partly hidden or the name is on the right page.

pages.full_text:
proprietor was, welcoming. He showed us the
bedroom up an outside staircase in a barn.
The supper was delicious. Cream of water
cress soup + lamb stew. Simon had meringue
glace + I had gritty chocolate. One of the
meriques at the end had licorice, yuk!
Breakfast was sumptuous. Oct 8th [?Von]
        We set off near the __Seine__ + caught
a ferry with the bikes to cycle to __Jumièges__
__Abbey__. Unfortunately the ferry was the wrong
ferry, so we had a 4 mile bike ride instead
of one. The abbey was very beautiful,
although the lantern had been dynamited
in 1796, for the owner/stonemason to use it
as a quarry. Such hatred of the church!
        Lovely Romanesque arches +
later Gothic arches. The ferry there was
from __Yainville__, Jumièges ferry back, +
we cycled through Heurteauville. One of the
roads is called "thatched house road".
The thatched house have irises growing
out of the roofridge. Many houses
are brown + white, as in Asterie.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '4' visible in top-right corner — this appears to be a sub-page number within a trip section rather than a sequential diary page number. After 'Oct 8th' there appears to be a word that could be '[?Von]' or possibly a place abbreviation — unclear. The diarist spells 'meriques' (meringues) and 'licorice'. 'Asterie' at the end is uncertain — could be 'Astérie' or a reference to the Asterix character or a place; spelled as written. Year cannot be confirmed from this page alone; the volume covers 1989–2003 and this is near the end (pdf_page 298 of 350), suggesting late 1990s or early 2000s, but no year is visible on the page.
